message:
fix: random_regular_graph converges instead of FailClosed (br-r37-c1-nzo8r)

fnx.random_regular_graph(d=4, n=10) raised ValueError(FailClosed{...})
for ~20% of seeds where nx.random_regular_graph succeeded. Failing
seeds in [0, 50) included 2, 3, 7, 9, 13, 33, 34, 42, 43, 44.

Root cause
----------
The Rust implementation in crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs used a
naive stub-pairing loop: shuffle stubs, pair them up, abort the
*entire* attempt on the first self-edge or duplicate, retry up to
100 times, then FailClosed. Many seed/(d,n) combinations cannot
converge under that strategy because at least one collision is near-
certain in any single shuffle.

nx (random_graphs.random_regular_graph._try_creation) instead
tracks unmatched stubs in a `potential_edges` map after each shuffle
pass and re-pairs only those, restarting the whole attempt only when
its `_suitable` predicate proves no remaining edge can be placed.
The retry-on-collision behavior makes a single attempt almost always
converge.

Fix
---
Port nx's smarter inner loop into the Rust generator:
- New helper `try_create_random_regular(rng, n, d)` mirrors nx's
  `_try_creation`: tracks `potential_edges: BTreeMap<usize, usize>`
  (BTreeMap for deterministic iteration), only fully restarts when
  `rrg_suitable` returns false.
- New helper `rrg_suitable(edges, potential_edges)` mirrors nx's
  `_suitable`: returns true if at least one valid edge can still
  be placed among unmatched stub pairs.
- The outer max_tries=100 cap is preserved as a safety bound; with
  the smarter inner loop the convergence rate is ~100% within a
  single attempt.

Results
-------
- Pre-fix d=4,n=10: 10/50 seeds FailClosed (20%).
- Post-fix d=4,n=10: 0/500 seeds FailClosed (0%); regularity
  verified across {(3,10),(4,10),(5,12),(6,20),(3,100),(8,50)} ×
  {seed=1,2,7,42,100}.
- Full test sweep: 6572 passed, 0 failed (was 6570 before — +2
  for new regression tests).

The fix does not affect exact-output parity with nx (already noted
in the existing br-codrgen comment as a separately tracked item):
the algorithm now matches nx structurally but the underlying RNG
shuffle order still differs from Python's random.Random.

Tests
-----
New TestRandomRegularConvergence in
tests/python/test_classic_generators.py:
- test_converges_for_previously_failing_seeds: explicitly exercises
  all ten historical FailClosed seeds and asserts d-regularity.
- test_converges_for_500_seeds_at_d4_n10: regression sweep — every
  seed in [0, 500) must succeed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mpv5x","title":"parity: __str__ drift on view classes and Graph (name dropped, repr-style instead of bare-data)","description":"Several __str__ implementations diverge from nx: NodeView/EdgeView/DegreeView/NodeDataView/EdgeDataView/AtlasView/AdjacencyView fall back to __repr__ (e.g. 'NodeView((0, 1, 2))') instead of nx's bare-data form ('[0, 1, 2]'). Graph.__str__ ignores the graph name attribute: fnx returns 'Graph with 0 nodes and 0 edges' even when G.graph['name'] = 'G' is set; nx returns 'Graph named \'G\' with 0 nodes and 0 edges'. Drop-in code that does print(G), print(G.nodes), or asserts on str(view) gets different output. Fix: add explicit __str__ methods that return the bare-data forms matching nx exactly; fix Graph.__str__ to include the name when set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:36:59.610629652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.211977409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.209976079Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 73c840f. View __str__ returns bare data (list/dict) matching nx; Graph.__str__ includes name when set. 15 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-nhz31","title":"test_coverage_gaps: refactor recent direct-nx wrappers + regenerate coverage.md","description":"Two pre-existing failures in test_coverage_gaps.py:
- test_public_coverage_has_no_networkx_delegated_exports: 54 exports classify as NX_DELEGATED. The test asserts == 0.
- test_generated_coverage_matrix_document_is_current: docs/coverage.md is out of date.

Most of the 54 NX_DELEGATED items pre-date my recent work. But my recent fixes added 6 more by directly importing nx in public functions:
- intersection (br-r37-c1-saf8a)
- k_truss (br-r37-c1-3qopf)
- maximum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- minimum_spanning_tree (br-r37-c1-rtak4)
- transitive_closure_dag (br-r37-c1-utmy6)
- transitive_reduction (br-r37-c1-utmy6)

The project convention (used by stochastic_block_model, hoffman_singleton_graph, etc.) is to keep the nx import inside a private _xxx_impl helper so the public function is classified as PY_WRAPPER instead of NX_DELEGATED.

Scope of this bead: refactor the 6 I recently introduced to follow the convention, and regenerate docs/coverage.md. The other 48 pre-existing items are out of scope (separate refactor).

After this bead, the count drops from 54 → 48, the second test passes if docs are regenerated, and the first test still fails but no worse than before.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:43.443005498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.770550620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:58:10.768479207Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ntkdn","title":"parity: random_sequence powerlaw/zipf/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 fns)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). Used by stochastic generators and degree-seq sanity checks. Add wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:04:43.303897526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.064336059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.062433818Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b08418c: powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence wrappers; all 4 verified against nx (n=20 exp=2.5 seed=42 powerlaw match exactly, cumulative_distribution = [0,0.5,0.8,1.0]).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-nzo8r","title":"random_regular_graph FailClosed for ~20% of seeds where nx succeeds","description":"fnx.random_regular_graph(d=4, n=10) raises ValueError(FailClosed{...}) for seeds 2,3,7,9,13,33,34,42,43,44 (10/50 = 20%) where nx succeeds. Root cause: crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs:670 uses a naive stub-pairing algorithm that throws away the entire attempt on any duplicate/self-edge and gives up after 100 attempts. nx's algorithm (see __init__.py:32986-33024) tracks unmatched stubs in potential_edges and only restarts on _suitable failure, which makes it converge in nearly all cases. Port nx's smarter inner loop into the Rust impl.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:23:16.888694411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:23:44.916043717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-o97vk","title":"cycle_graph / wheel_graph adj-iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.cycle_graph(n) and fnx.wheel_graph(n) (and likely sibling Rust-backed generators) produce a graph whose adj-iteration order differs from nx for the closing edge (the one that wraps back to node 0).

Repro:
  cycle_graph(4):
    fnx adj[3] = [0, 2]
    nx  adj[3] = [2, 0]
  cycle_graph(6):
    fnx adj[5] = [0, 4]
    nx  adj[5] = [4, 0]
  wheel_graph(5):
    fnx adj[4] = [0, 1, 3]
    nx  adj[4] = [0, 3, 1]

The same graph constructed manually via add_nodes_from + add_edge in the same order matches nx exactly, so the drift is in the Rust _rust_cycle_graph / _rust_wheel_graph generators which canonicalise/sort their adj insertion. The Python add_edge path preserves nx's contract.

Drop-in code that iterated adj[node] of a generator-built graph drifted (e.g. for visualising edge order, BFS/DFS-strategy tie-breaking).

Fix: route cycle_graph / wheel_graph to the Python construction path (which already matches nx) instead of _rust_*. Other generators that pass earlier probes (path, star, complete) already match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:29:01.392150888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:32:32.922772590Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:32:32.920424293Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ohxpp","title":"parity: edge_boundary returns list (not generator), descendants_at_distance returns frozenset (not set)","description":"Two return-type drifts found in extended sweep: (1) edge_boundary(G, nbunch1, nbunch2=None) returns list while nx returns generator — same iter contract issue as br-r37-c1-682kr. (2) descendants_at_distance(G, source, distance) returns frozenset while nx returns set. Drop-in code calling .add()/.remove() on the result breaks since frozenset is immutable. Fix: convert edge_boundary to a generator (yield from); coerce descendants_at_distance result to set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:59:57.136602866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:02:25.009360090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:02:25.007371375Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit d9a9279. edge_boundary now returns a true generator; descendants_at_distance returns a mutable set. 11 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-oiic9","title":"parity: all_pairs_node_connectivity flow_func ignored + iteration order","description":"fnx.all_pairs_node_connectivity(G, nbunch=None, flow_func=None) accepts flow_func but ignores it (always uses default max-flow). Also outer/inner dict iteration order differs from networkx for K4: fnx outer order is 1,0,2,3 while nx is 0,1,2,3. Honor flow_func by delegating to nx when provided, and stabilize iteration order (use G.nodes() insertion order). Located in python/franken_networkx/__init__.py near the existing all_pairs_node_connectivity wrapper.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:58:20.898200931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:05:56.920452351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T05:05:56.918586339Z","close_reason":"Completed in f14208d","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -828,6 +828,44 @@ def test_odd_n_times_d_raises_networkx_error(self):
             fnx.random_regular_graph(3, 5, seed=0)
 
 
+# br-r37-c1-nzo8r: pre-fix the Rust random_regular_graph used a naive
+# "any duplicate/self-edge throws away the whole attempt" loop that capped
+# at 100 attempts. For (d=4, n=10) ~20% of seeds in [0, 50) raised
+# ValueError(FailClosed{...}) — seeds 2,3,7,9,13,33,34,42,43,44 — while
+# nx.random_regular_graph succeeded for the same inputs because nx tracks
+# unmatched stubs in `potential_edges` and only restarts when no remaining
+# edge can be placed. The fix ports nx's smarter inner loop into the Rust
+# generator. This test guards against regression.
+class TestRandomRegularConvergence:
+    def test_converges_for_previously_failing_seeds(self):
+        # All ten seeds historically observed to FailClosed under the naive
+        # algorithm must now succeed and yield a valid 4-regular graph on
+        # 10 nodes.
+        for seed in (2, 3, 7, 9, 13, 33, 34, 42, 43, 44):
+            g = fnx.random_regular_graph(4, 10, seed=seed)
+            assert g.number_of_nodes() == 10
+            degrees = [g.degree(v) for v in g.nodes()]
+            assert all(deg == 4 for deg in degrees), (
+                f"seed={seed}: expected all degrees == 4, got {degrees}"
+            )
+            for u, v in g.edges():
+                assert u != v, f"seed={seed}: self-loop ({u},{v})"
+
+    def test_converges_for_500_seeds_at_d4_n10(self):
+        # Broad sweep: zero failures across a 500-seed window. Pre-fix the
+        # failure rate was ~20%.
+        failures = []
+        for seed in range(500):
+            try:
+                fnx.random_regular_graph(4, 10, seed=seed)
+            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - regression sentinel
+                failures.append(seed)
+        assert not failures, (
+            f"random_regular_graph(4, 10) failed for {len(failures)} of 500 "
+            f"seeds: first 10 = {failures[:10]}"
+        )
+
+
 # br-badiacritic: nx uses Unicode "Barabási–Albert" in its error messages
 # for barabasi_albert_graph and dual_barabasi_albert_graph (but ASCII
 # "Barabasi-Albert" for extended_barabasi_albert_graph). fnx matches
